title: Cathode.
kicker: 57 original values · nine themes · AAA at full brightness
tagline: Phosphor on dark glass, one live wire of cyan. Where inkwell absorbs light, cathode emits it — a color system for agentic products that ship signal, and can prove it.

principles.title: Emission, not absorption.
principles.body: Inkwell is ink soaking into paper. Cathode is phosphor firing at glass. Same rigor, opposite direction — built to be noticed and still be trusted.

principles.title: One live wire.
principles.body: Volt is the only interactive current. If it's cyan, it conducts; if it doesn't conduct, it is never cyan. Voltage stays precious or it stops meaning anything.

principles.title: State is a lamp.
principles.body: Pulse, flare and klaxon are indicator lights — lit only when the machine has something to report. A lamp that is always on reports nothing.

principles.title: Day is the guest.
principles.body: Glass is the default; daylight is the away kit. Both first-class, both measured — but this system was born after dark.

rampNotes: Five ramps, fifty-seven live values. Every value is original — zero hexes shared with inkwell — and every ramp is luminance-monotonic, end to end.
roleNotes: Components speak in roles, never in hexes. Each role resolves per channel — ship the tokens and these decisions travel with them.
